notes: Hammond: 'TP1, Debit-Credit and Grey (or Gray?) are all names for the same benchmark'
Mascall warns spec leaves scope for 'artistic interpretation' by vendors
DEC DBS adopted End-to-End Qualified and Peak as standard for FY88 corporate reporting
Internal use TPS/VUP; competitive comparison switches to TPS/MIP
Rudy Downs hired as DEC DBS Performance Manager; would later own metric evolution
Saghagen: 'We and Wang are the only remaining vendors' for large finance-company deal
Anon Et Al credited authorship — widely understood to be Jim Gray and collaborators
Kittell: 'Unqualified specs (just a TPS value by itself) are worthless'
Mandated FY87 reporting format for all DEC DBS publications
Standard back-up rate metric introduced; minimum disk/back-up combination required
Physical I/Os per transaction defined as second yardstick of DB technology
